title: Lightly Deep-Fried Ethiopian Kielbasa Tod
description: A quick-fix Ethiopian-inspired dish featuring kielbasa, deep-fried tod, and a hint of spices. This recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner or lunch.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan or a deep fryer to 350°F (180°C).
Cut the kielbasa into 1-inch pieces and set aside.
In a shallow dish, mix together the flour, cornstarch, sambal oelek, salt, and black pepper.
Dip each kielbasa piece into the beaten eggs and then coat in the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Fry the coated kielbasa pieces in batches until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per batch.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the fried kielbasa from the oil and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the fried kielbasa hot with a side of tod (Ethiopian flatbread) for dipping in the spicy oil.
